name: PLS Check Cashers
description_short: PLS Check Cashers is a 24/7 financial services chain in 12 states offering check cashing, money orders, bill pay, DMV services, and prepaid debit cards.
description_long:
PLS Financial Services, Inc. operates PLS 247 locations branded as Community Currency Exchanges across 12 states: Arizona, California, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Massachusetts, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Texas, and Wisconsin. The company has been a fixture in underbanked urban communities for decades, positioning itself as a full-service financial hub for consumers who rely on alternative financial services rather than traditional banks.

PLS offers an unusually broad range of services under one roof. On the financial side, customers can cash checks, purchase money orders, pay bills, send international remittances via Ria Money Transfer, access government benefits, and exchange foreign currency. PLS also issues the Xpectations! PLUS Debit Mastercard — a prepaid card product with an accompanying mobile app — giving unbanked customers a card-based payment option. For businesses, PLS offers a dedicated PLS For Business suite of services.

What sets PLS apart from typical check cashers is the inclusion of government and transportation services. Many locations handle vehicle registration and renewals, title transfers, license plate issuance, and city stickers and transit cards — services normally handled at the DMV or city offices. Combined with 24/7 operating hours across all service categories, PLS functions as a one-stop shop for consumers who need financial and government transactions handled outside standard business hours. Supplementary services like notary, fax and copy, ATM access, and phone top-ups round out the offering.

PLS fills a genuine need for the unbanked and underbanked, and the 24/7 availability is a real differentiator. However, the website does not disclose any fee schedule for check cashing or other services, which makes cost comparison impossible before visiting a store. Check cashing fees at businesses like PLS typically run 1–3% of the check face value, which adds up quickly for workers cashing regular paychecks. Consumers with access to a bank account or credit union will almost always find a cheaper alternative for most of these transactions.
pros: Open 24/7 for all service categories — financial, DMV, and business
Handles vehicle registration, title transfers, and license plates alongside financial services
Ria Money Transfer available for international remittances
Xpectations! PLUS Debit Mastercard gives unbanked customers a prepaid card option with mobile app
Notary services available for wills, deeds, power of attorney, and other legal documents
12-state footprint with multiple locations per metro area
cons: No fee schedule published on the website — pricing is opaque before visiting
Check cashing fees are typically higher than using a bank or credit union
No credit products, savings accounts, or FDIC-insured deposits — not a banking replacement
Services vary by location; not every store offers every service listed
No full-featured online or app-based account management beyond the prepaid Xpectations card
best_for: Unbanked consumers who need to cash paychecks or government checks without a bank account
Immigrants and workers sending money abroad via Ria Money Transfer
People who need DMV or city government transactions handled evenings, weekends, or overnight
Night shift workers or anyone whose schedule conflicts with standard bank and government office hours
diagnosis: PLS is best suited for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need to cash checks, pay bills, or handle DMV transactions outside of normal business hours — particularly those in urban markets where PLS has a dense store presence. The main caveat is that PLS does not publish its fee schedule online, and check cashing fees at alternative financial service providers are typically much higher than free or low-cost options at banks and credit unions, making PLS a convenience-driven rather than cost-efficient choice.
